mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future cash flows, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates significantly, reducing corporate earnings growth and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer confidence and credit availability, creating a compounding demand shock that pushes equity markets higher over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate revenues and generates positive short-term equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during inflationary supply bottlenecks, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO momentum (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 255.0, inflation expectations accelerate, prompting markets to price in tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, resulting in negative forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 255.0, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation is supply-driven rather than demand-driven.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment indicates labor market tightening and wage growth, boosting consumer spending and corporate profits, which drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when wage growth fails to outpace inflation, or quarterly returns are negative in >55% of divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand and economic momentum, which directly supports corporate earnings trajectories and generates positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or GDP growth momentum shows no statistically significant lead-lag relationship with quarterly equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 90.0, manufacturing contraction signals weakening economic activity and reduced capacity utilization, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative short-term equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >65% of instances where INDPRO drops below 90.0, or the threshold effect is statistically indistinguishable from noise.
